repo_prior_art.cite: ThmSmith doc/API.md section 3 says ThmSmith/PartialID is empty; closest pid_late_bounded_defiers is one-period CLATE/bounded-defier, not dynamic full-history

published_prior_art.cite: DuarteFinkelsteinKnoxMummoloShpitser2024: generic automated sharp bounds for discrete causal settings subsume finite latent-response programming, but not this model-specific full-history statement

repo_prior_art.cite: pid_late_bounded_defiers: one-period Balke-Pearl/LATE endpoint; ExactID/DynamicLATE cited by proposal is exact-ID rather than this partial-ID LP

published_prior_art.cite: BalkePearl1997 plus standard sequential-randomization/IPW logic: the theorem is correctly presented as a sanity reduction, not a novelty claim

closest are DuarteFinkelsteinKnoxMummoloShpitser2024, ChenZhang2023, Han2024, MichaelCuiLorchTchetgen2024, VanDenBergBonevMammen2020; none states strict full-history-vs-Markovized sharp-bound separation
BalkePearl1997 gives one static table; ChenZhang2023 uses Bellman partial-ID language, but neither gives a global nonpasteability theorem for local BP certificates

repo_prior_art.cite: ExactID/DynamicLATE is a two-period exact-ID comparator under stronger assumptions, not this T<=2 no-separation partial-ID minimality claim

published_prior_art.cite: SwansonLabrecqueHernan2018 and ChenZhang2023 are relevant dynamic-IV comparators, but the proposal has not yet anchored the T<=2 minimality survey tightly

novelty_flags.label: Conjecture 3
novelty_flags.one_line: The named checklist gives too little targeted published prior art for the T<=2 minimality claim; add explicit two-stage dynamic-IV and finite-horizon partial-ID comparators, not only the sustained-null paper.

soundness_flags.label: Conjecture 2
soundness_flags.one_line: The local Balke-Pearl table uses a binary terminal response y_d after 'marginalizing over the remaining realized continuation'; after period t in a T=3 process the continuation is stochastic and history-dependent, so this object is not a well-defined binary local response without fixing a continuation policy or replacing it by a bounded mean.

strengthening_paths.suggestion: Repair Conjecture 2 by defining the local response table conditional on a fixed continuation regime/path, or by changing y_d to a [0,1] continuation-averaged mean and adjusting the local polytope accordingly.

For Conjecture 1, add a small symbolic LP certificate template: name the two histories, list the compressed constraints dropped by A_s, and identify which dual inequality creates the strict lower-bound gap.
Strengthen the Conjecture 3 literature comparison with Han 2024, Chen-Zhang 2023, and the two-stage exact-ID DynamicLATE catalogue result as explicit T<=2 boundary comparators.

recommended_next_step: Revise the local-to-global pasting definition first; the main full-history novelty survives, but Conjecture 2 is not yet mathematically well-posed.
